title: KDNA Source Authority Graph (SAG)
description: Declares, per source, its type, authority level, scope, and conflict-resolution precedence. Authoring-time only; never enters runtime .kdna container. See RFC-0013 §3.1.

sag_id.description: Stable identifier for this SAG instance, e.g., sag_<domain>_<yyyy_mm_dd>.

domain_id.description: The KDNA domain this SAG belongs to, e.g., @aikdna/writing.

version_intent.description: The target domain version this SAG is intended for, e.g., 4.0.0-draft.1.

sources.description: All sources that contributed to or constrain the current judgment. At least one source is required.

id.description: Unique identifier for this source within the SAG, referenced by precedence_order and conflict_policies.

type.description: Kind of source. human_locked_charter and author_confirmation carry the highest semantic weight.

authority.description: Authority level per RFC-0013 §3.1. current_highest is override-capable; deprecated must not be used as positive evidence.

status.description: Lifecycle status of this source. stable_but_superseded means it remains referenced but is not the highest.

can_override.description: Whether this source can override lower-authority sources in conflict resolution. Only current_highest sources should set this to true.

scope.description: Which part of the domain this source constrains, e.g., full_book, axioms_and_ontology, open_questions, terminology. Free-form string is allowed because domains differ.

notes.description: Optional human-readable notes about this source.

precedence_order.description: Ordered list of source.id values, highest precedence first. When two sources conflict, the earlier entry wins. Every entry MUST match a source.id in 'sources'.

conflict_policies.description: Optional explicit policies for known conflict patterns. Each policy is a rule that the S5 Human Lock gate can verify.

when.description: Description of the conflict pattern, e.g., published_work_conflicts_with_human_locked_charter.

resolution.description: How to resolve the conflict, e.g., charter_overrides, charter_overrides_and_record_rename, surface_to_human.

record_required.description: Whether the resolution must be recorded in a designated location for audit.

record_target.description: Where the resolution record is written, e.g., candidate_cards.jsonl.conflict_resolution.

sensitivity.description: Optional sensitivity metadata. Does not gate schema validation but is read by PR-3 Studio gates.
